# tools/parity/dump_kokoro_reference.py. `key = value`;
# list values are space-separated.
# mode = placeholder: the Rust parity harness runs shape /
# length checks only (byte-level parity is a follow-up).
# mode = full: byte-level parity vs a native NumPy re-forward.
model = hexgrad/Kokoro-82M
checkpoint_file = kokoro-v1_0.pth
seed = 1234
atol = 0.01
mode = placeholder
vocab_size = 256
hidden_dim = 512
style_dim = 128
voice_id = 0

num_phonemes = 24
enc_pos = 8
dec_frames = 64
prosody_channels = 2
mel_channels = 80
pcm_len = 16000
